title: ChatGPT Business release notes: Codex Remote generally available with QR pairing
author: OpenAI Help Center
why_it_matters: OpenAI release notes position Codex Remote as a broader mobile and desktop collaboration entry rather than a niche experiment.
actionable_takeaway: Use iPhone as the capture and steering surface, then review evidence and publish from Mac.

title: Codex changelog: iOS task management, diff controls, and usage-limit messaging
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The changelog shows Codex task creation, search, opening, forking, management, and review controls moving into mobile workflows.
actionable_takeaway: Restrict phone use to lightweight task creation, search, steering, and approval prompts; keep final verification on Mac.

title: OpenAI release notes: Workspace Agent token-based billing
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: Workspace Agent work is becoming measurable by token use, which makes recurring agent workflows easier to evaluate by cost and output.
actionable_takeaway: Track source cards, top signals, knowledge cards, validation commands, and deployment result for every daily run.

title: OpenAI track: Building agents
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The official track separates simple agents, multi-agent orchestration, memory, guardrails, tools, and evaluation.
actionable_takeaway: Start with the smallest single-agent workflow; split into subagents only when roles and outputs are naturally different.

title: AGENTS.md interoperability guide
author: Yurukusa
why_it_matters: The guide frames AGENTS.md as a shared instruction layer for Codex, Claude Code, Jules, Cursor, and other coding agents.
actionable_takeaway: Treat AGENTS.md as the repository's interpreter: include task boundaries, tests, safety limits, and response format once.

title: AGENTS.md public specification site
author: AGENTS.md community
why_it_matters: A public specification makes agent instructions easier to standardize across tools and repositories.
actionable_takeaway: Keep the local AGENTS.md short, explicit, and testable so multiple tools can follow the same rules.

title: OpenAI Codex product page
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: The current Codex product page remains the baseline for explaining delegated coding tasks, environment setup, and review expectations.
actionable_takeaway: When reporting a Codex workflow, include the task, environment, evidence, and handoff result instead of only a narrative summary.

title: OpenAI Status dashboard
author: OpenAI Status
why_it_matters: Recurring daily automation depends on external services such as ChatGPT, OpenAI APIs, GitHub, and Vercel; availability must be visible.
actionable_takeaway: If publish fails, keep local files, report the failing dependency, and avoid destructive retries.

title: Multi-Agent Claude Code
author: Terp AI Labs
why_it_matters: The repo is a useful public reference for role-based agent orchestration patterns, even if it is not a direct Codex dependency.
actionable_takeaway: Use role splits like collect, verify, translate, and publish only after a single-agent run shows repeated bottlenecks.

title: Codex Micro keyboard for Codex workflows
author: Work Louder
why_it_matters: Physical controls around Codex suggest a new workflow pattern: repeated AI commands becoming muscle-memory actions.
actionable_takeaway: Before buying hardware, map the repeated commands first: start task, paste source, run checks, open deploy, archive card.

title: ChatGPT widgets and iPhone Shortcuts example
author: Paul Solt
why_it_matters: The guide shows how mobile widgets and Shortcuts can become quick-entry surfaces for ChatGPT-style workflows.
actionable_takeaway: Prototype a shortcut that captures a URL and appends it to the next AI Radar input queue without public posting.

title: Awesome AI Agents 2026
author: Zijian Ni / contributors
why_it_matters: A curated landscape helps spot recurring agent categories, but individual claims must be checked against primary sources.
actionable_takeaway: Use the list for discovery only; promote items to AI Radar only after primary-source verification.
